response: The coach who was replaced in 1996 as the longest tenured active coach in both the NBA and all US major sports leagues was **Gregg Popovich**. He was the head coach of the San Antonio Spurs and took over in 1996. Prior to this, **Bob Hill** was the head coach of the San Antonio Spurs, and he was replaced by Gregg Popovich. Popovich became the longest tenured active coach in both the NBA and all US major sports leagues, a record he holds to this day.
